summary: Reed's conjecture $\chi(G) \le \lceil \tfrac{1}{2}(\Delta(G)+1+\omega(G)) \rceil$ remains open for general graphs. Since the 2007 OPG posting, it has been established for quasi-line graphs and further structural classes, and the quantitative 'epsilon version' $\chi(G) \le (1-\varepsilon)(\Delta+1) + \varepsilon\,\omega$ has been progressively strengthened from $\varepsilon \approx 1/130{,}000$ to $\varepsilon = 1/26$ and reportedly further to $\varepsilon \approx 0.119$.

since_posted.title: A local strengthening of Reed's ω, Δ, χ conjecture for quasi-line graphs
since_posted.authors: Maria Chudnovsky, Andrew D. King, Matthieu Plumettaz, Paul Seymour
since_posted.claim: Proves a local strengthening of Reed's conjecture for all quasi-line graphs (including line graphs), along with polytime colouring algorithms.

since_posted.title: A note on Reed's Conjecture about ω, Δ and χ with respect to vertices of high degree
since_posted.claim: Proves Reed's conjecture for graphs in which all vertices of degree ≥ 5 form a stable set, and for graphs in which every odd induced cycle contains a vertex of degree ≤ 3.

since_posted.title: Colouring Graphs with Sparse Neighbourhoods: Bounds and Applications
since_posted.authors: Marthe Bonamy, Thomas Perrett, Luke Postle
since_posted.claim: Dramatically improves Reed's epsilon theorem by showing the bound χ(G) ≤ (1−ε)(Δ+1) + ε·ω holds for large Δ with ε = 1/26, up from the prior ε ≈ 1/130,000.

since_posted.title: A local epsilon version of Reed's Conjecture
since_posted.venue: Journal of Combinatorial Theory, Series B
since_posted.claim: Proves a local list-colouring epsilon version of Reed's conjecture, establishing the bound under mild local density conditions.

since_posted.title: A Recolouring Version of a Conjecture of Reed
since_posted.authors: Lucas De Meyer, Clément Legrand-Duchesne, Jared León, Tim Planken, Youri Tamitegama
since_posted.claim: Studies a Kempe-recolouring variant of Reed's conjecture; proves the analogous bound with ε = 1/2 is achievable for odd-hole-free graphs (and tight up to one colour).

search_queries: Reed's conjecture chi omega Delta graph coloring progress proof 2010 2020 2025
Reed conjecture chromatic number clique number maximum degree partial results survey arXiv
Reed conjecture "epsilon" "locally sparse" chromatic number Bonamy Delcourt Postle Hurley 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024
notes: The conjecture remains open for general graphs. An important unverified result: Hurley, de Verclos, and Kang (SODA 2021) reportedly improved the epsilon to ε ≈ 0.119 ('An improved procedure for colouring graphs of bounded local density'), but this paper was not fetched within the query budget and cannot be cited. The digraph analogue by Kawarabayashi–Picasarri-Arrieta (arXiv 2407.05827, 2024) is not included in since_posted as it concerns directed graphs. The SIAM journal publication of the Chudnovsky et al. paper (DOI 10.1137/110847585 per search results) is likely 2012–2013; only the 2011 arXiv submission was directly verified.
